perturbation.original_text: Terms of payment will be 1/3 upon placement of order and 2/3 balance net thirty (30) days or as mutually agreed upon in writing between the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: Terms of payment will be 1/3 upon placement of order and 2/3 balance net sixty (60) days. Any agreement to change this must be made in writing and acknowledged by both parties through notarization.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the payment deadline. Initially, the balance was due in 30 days or as mutually agreed upon, making it flexible. The modified text sets a firm 60-day deadline and introduces a strict notarization requirement for any change, contradicting the original flexibility and enforceability of verbal agreements. This directly impacts how 'Prices and Terms' are interpreted and enforced.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Prices and Terms. The price for the Product as set forth in Section 9 herein, sold by SIERRA to ENVISION, shall be subject to change due to changes in manufacturing costs and so as to maximize profits; any changes in price for the Product shall not be applicable to previously accepted orders and shall be made with at least ninety (90) days advance notice in writing and in good faith by conference of the parties. ENVISION shall not resell the Product alone. Terms of payment will be 1/3 upon placement of order and 2/3 balance net sixty (60) days. Any agreement to change this must be made in writing and acknowledged by both parties through notarization. Delivery will be F.O.B. ENVISION shall be responsible for all costs of shipping from SIERRA to ENVISION.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the payment terms by setting a firm 60-day deadline with a notarization requirement for any changes, contradicting the original flexibility that allowed for mutually agreed-upon changes to the payment deadline. The contradicted text now contains both the original, more flexible payment terms AND the stricter, notarized-change-only terms in the changed text. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding the actual payment terms, as the document now simultaneously states two different and incompatible rules.

perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement shall be two (2) years from the Effective Date with automatic annual renewals thereafter provided either party does not provide sixty (60) days notice of termination prior to the renewal date or the Agreement is not otherwise terminated as set forth in Section 8.
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement shall be two (2) years from the Effective Date with automatic annual renewals thereafter provided that SIERRA provides sixty (60) days notice of termination prior to the renewal date or the Agreement is not otherwise terminated as set forth in Section 8.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates a contradiction related to the termination clause. The original text allows either party to terminate with 60 days' notice. The modified text restricts this right only to SIERRA, removing ENVISION's ability to terminate the agreement under the same conditions, thus creating an imbalance and contradiction in rights. This contradicts the mutual termination rights as initially conceived, creating ambiguity on whether ENVISION can terminate before the annual renewal.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Term. The term of this Agreement shall be two (2) years from the Effective Date with automatic annual renewals thereafter provided that SIERRA provides sixty (60) days notice of termination prior to the renewal date or the Agreement is not otherwise terminated as set forth in Section 8.
perturbation.justification: The original text allows either party (SIERRA or ENVISION) to terminate the agreement with 60 days' notice. The changed text restricts this right only to SIERRA. The contradicted text also states that only SIERRA can terminate with 60 days notice. While the changed text and contradicted text align in restricting termination rights to only SIERRA, the original text granted termination rights to both parties, thus creating a contradiction in relation to that. The change creates an imbalance and contradiction in rights as described in the Perturbation Explanation, as ENVISION is no longer able to terminate. Therefore, the changed text introduces a contradiction, and we should assign 'YES'.
